us-politics.headline: Trump Presses Iran With Deadline, Toll Threats, and God
us-politics.body:
Trump held a news conference doubling down on a Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet U.S. demands, threatening wider bombing of Iranian infrastructure if talks collapse. He suggested the U.S. could charge tolls for passage through the Strait of Hormuz - a move with no clear legal precedent - and invoked God's support for the American cause. Critics, including some Democrats, raised war crimes concerns after strikes on civilian-adjacent targets. Trump brushed those questions aside.

OPEC+ announced it would boost oil output once the Strait reopens, signaling markets are already pricing in a prolonged conflict. The Pentagon canceled a scheduled press briefing with Hegseth and General Caine, offering no explanation. The looming deadline has sharpened divisions: supporters see maximum pressure working, while opponents warn of an unplanned escalation with no clear exit strategy.

sources.title: Trump says God supports U.S. cause in Iran war as he threatens wider bombing
5 takeaways from Trump’s news conference on Iran
Trump’s looming deadline for Iran
OPEC+ agrees to boost output when Strait of Hormuz reopens
Trump suggests US could charge toll for Strait of Hormuz passage
Trump brushes off questions about potential war crimes in Iran
Trump doubles down on Tuesday night deadline for Iran to meet US demands
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth

us-politics.headline: Hegseth Faces Impeachment Push, Ivy League Purge
us-politics.body:
House Democrat Nazzi Ansari introduced articles of impeachment against Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, citing his conduct around the Iran conflict. The move has little chance of success in a Republican-controlled House but signals growing Democratic pressure on the Pentagon's civilian leadership.

Meanwhile, Hegseth moved to cut military education ties with Ivy League institutions, replacing them with Hillsdale College - a conservative Michigan school with no existing military program infrastructure. Supporters call it a values-driven correction to ideological drift in officer training. Critics argue it trades academic rigor for political alignment. The canceled Pentagon press briefing added to questions about transparency at the Defense Department during an active military operation.

sources.title: House Democrat says she’ll move to impeach Hegseth over Iran war
Hegseth cuts Ivy League ties in military education shake-up, taps Hillsdale
Pentagon cancels Tuesday press briefing with Caine, Hegseth

us-politics.headline: 2026 Midterms Take Shape: Money, Primaries, and Polling
us-politics.body:
A Republican super PAC announced a $342 million investment in 2026 Senate races, while a separate AI-backed PAC claimed early wins in GOP primaries and said it is expanding into more contests. Montana Republican Kurt Alme, handpicked by Senator Steve Daines, entered the 2026 Senate race vowing to hold the seat. Former Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin told a podcast he has "more to give," keeping his political future open.

On the Democratic side, Virginia Governor-elect Abigail Spanberger's approval numbers are sliding amid backlash over a gerrymandering dispute, according to a new poll. New York City mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani unveiled a racial equity plan that drew an immediate pushback letter from the Justice Department. The early jockeying underscores how both parties are already in campaign mode more than a year before Election Day.

sources.title: GOP super PAC announces $342M investment in midterm Senate races
AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
Steve Daines' handpicked Senate successor Kurt Alme vows to keep Montana in Republican hands in 2026
Youngkin on political future: ‘I have more to give’
New poll reveals Spanberger's popularity is plummeting amid backlash over gerrymandering
Mamdani unveils new 'racial equity plan' for more 'equitable future' that prompts quick DOJ pushback

us-politics.headline: Trump Clashes With Courts, EPA Rolls Back Methane Rules
us-politics.body:
Trump publicly attacked the Supreme Court over its handling of his birthright citizenship executive order and tariff challenges, accusing the justices of overreach. The criticism is unusual in its directness and reflects broader administration frustration with judicial checks on its agenda.

The EPA took an early procedural step toward rolling back Biden-era methane flaring rules, a move welcomed by the oil and gas industry and opposed by environmental groups who say it will increase emissions. Separately, the Education Department rescinded Title IX agreements with schools that had extended protections based on gender identity, reverting to a narrower sex-based definition. Each action advances a deregulatory and socially conservative agenda that supporters call a correction and opponents call a rollback of hard-won protections.

sources.title: Trump rips Supreme Court over birthright citizenship, tariffs
EPA takes early step in methane rule rollback
Education Department rescinds previous Title IX agreements with schools over gender identity

au-politics.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith Arrested Over Afghanistan War Crimes
au-politics.body:
Australian Federal Police arrested decorated soldier Ben Roberts-Smith at Sydney Airport on Monday over alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan. The AFP commissioner confirmed the arrest publicly, marking a significant escalation after years of civil and criminal proceedings surrounding the Victoria Cross recipient.

The Australian War Memorial has announced it will review its display honouring Roberts-Smith following the charges. The Greens argue no one is above the law. Pauline Hanson has taken the opposite position, saying she will not abandon him.

The case divides opinion sharply. Supporters point to his military service and the presumption of innocence. Critics say accountability must apply regardless of rank or decoration. No conviction has been recorded.

sources.title: Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display following war crimes charges
Pauline Hanson says she won’t ‘abandon’ Ben Roberts-Smith as Greens argue ‘no one should be above the law’
Details of Ben Roberts-Smith arrest revealed by AFP commissioner - video
Afternoon Update: Ben Roberts-Smith arrested over alleged war crimes; Trump’s Iran deadline nears; and the appeal of barefoot hiking
Ben Roberts-Smith arrested by Australian federal police at Sydney airport – video

au-politics.headline: Queensland Crime Laws Face Scrutiny as New Data Released
au-politics.body:
Queensland's tough 'adult crime, adult time' laws have not resulted in a single child being charged under the expanded provisions in 11 years, according to government statistics. The data undermines a central argument used to justify the legislation - that harsher sentencing would deter serious youth offending.

Separately, new statewide crime figures show offences per capita have fallen, complicating the political narrative that crime is worsening. A Townsville crime spree over Easter, in which police were injured and cars allegedly stolen, drew local attention and renewed calls for stronger enforcement.

The tension between headline crime incidents and broader statistical trends reflects a persistent gap between public perception and data - one that both major parties have struggled to navigate honestly.

sources.title: No children charged with Queensland’s new ‘adult crime, adult time’ offences in 11 years, government statistics show
Townsville man counts cost of crime spree coming 'at a bad time'
New data on crime in Queensland shows drop in offences per capita

au-politics.headline: Record EV Sales as Fuel Crisis Reshapes Consumer Behaviour
au-politics.body:
Australians purchased a record number of new electric vehicles in March, with new sales data pointing directly to the recent fuel crisis as a driver of the shift. Rising petrol prices and supply anxiety appear to have accelerated a consumer trend that had been building gradually.

Tourist hotspots reported mixed impacts over Easter, with some visitors deterred by fuel uncertainty and others pushing through. The spike in EV uptake adds pressure to infrastructure planning, particularly in regional areas where charging networks remain thin.

The Greens have proposed expanded public transport investment as a parallel response. The Spectator has criticised that plan as impractical. The debate over how Australia transitions away from petrol dependency is now firmly in the election-year conversation.

sources.title: News live: Australians buy record number of new EVs in March; Australian War Memorial to review Ben Roberts-Smith display
The Green party’s transport plan is pure madness
Tourist hotspots share how fuel crisis impacted long weekend visitors
New EV sales data proves fuel crisis sparks consumer shift

au-politics.headline: Trump's Iran Deadline Looms as Rhetoric Shifts Daily
au-politics.body:
Donald Trump's posture toward Iran has oscillated sharply, moving between threats of destruction and claims he is 'not worried about it' within days. ABC Australia reports the shifting rhetoric is creating uncertainty among allies and analysts about whether military action is genuinely imminent.

The Spectator argues Trump's threat is detailed and credible, pointing to specific military positioning as evidence the administration is serious. Others read the same signals as negotiating pressure rather than a prelude to strikes.

For Australia, the stakes are indirect but real. Any conflict in the Gulf would affect energy markets and alliance obligations. The government has not publicly staked out a position on the standoff.

sources.title: Trump’s threat to destroy Iran is detailed and credible
'I'm not worried about it': Donald Trump's shifting rhetoric on the Iran war

world.headline: US-Israeli War on Iran Enters Critical Phase at Day 39
world.body:
Thirty-nine days into US-Israeli strikes on Iran, the conflict is escalating sharply as Trump's deadline for a Strait of Hormuz deal approaches with no breakthrough in sight. Israel has warned Iranians to avoid trains and railways, signalling imminent infrastructure strikes, while Trump has threatened attacks on power plants. A projectile struck a synagogue in Tehran, deepening the crisis.

Iran shows no sign of agreeing to a ceasefire, with analysts questioning why it would given the track record of both Washington and Tel Aviv. The Houthis are weighing whether to formally join the fight. Trump has floated charging ships for Hormuz passage, a move that would reshape global energy markets. Pakistan's solar expansion is cushioning it from the worst of the oil shock.

sources.title: What is driving the Houthis’ decision-making on joining the Iran war
How Pakistan’s solar boom is shielding it from worst of Iran war crisis
Iran war: What is happening on day 39 of US-Israeli attacks?
Israel threatens Iran’s trains, railways before Trump’s deadline expires

world.headline: Israel Strikes Lebanon and Gaza as Regional Violence Widens
world.body:
Israeli forces struck near a Gaza school, killing at least ten Palestinians, as military operations continued across multiple fronts. In the West Bank, soldiers fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a raid in Hebron. Protests erupted outside the US embassy in Tel Aviv, with demonstrators demanding an end to the wars.

In Lebanon, Israeli attacks are deliberately stoking internal divisions, according to residents and analysts. The strategy appears designed to fracture communities along sectarian lines rather than simply degrade military capacity. Meanwhile, a former Russian governor of Kursk was jailed in a corruption probe linked to Ukraine's cross-border incursion, a reminder that multiple conflicts continue to grind forward beyond the Iran theatre.

sources.title: ‘They want to create a rift’: Israeli attacks deepen Lebanon fissures
Protesters swarm US embassy in Tel Aviv, demanding end of wars
Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron
Russia jails former Kursk governor in Ukraine incursion-linked graft probe
Israeli air strike kills at least 10 Palestinians near Gaza school

world.headline: Australia Charges War Hero Roberts-Smith Over Afghan Killings
world.body:
Ben Roberts-Smith, Australia's most decorated living soldier, has been charged with war crimes allegedly committed in Afghanistan. The charges follow years of civil litigation in which a court found he had unlawfully killed Afghan civilians. His arrest marks the first time Australia has moved to criminally prosecute a soldier of his profile.

The case tests whether democracies can hold elite military figures accountable under the same laws applied to others. It comes as international scrutiny of conduct during the Afghanistan war remains intense across multiple allied nations.

sources.title: Australia's most-decorated living soldier charged over alleged war crimes
Australia charges ex-soldier Roberts-Smith with Afghan war crimes

world.headline: Vance Backs Orban, Taiwan Opposition Meets Xi in Beijing
world.body:
US Vice President JD Vance travelled to Budapest to publicly support Viktor Orban ahead of Hungary's Sunday election, a rare direct intervention by a senior American official in a European vote. The visit signals the Trump administration's continued alignment with nationalist governments inside the EU.

In Asia, Taiwan's opposition leader arrived in China for an expected meeting with Xi Jinping, a visit that will alarm Taipei's ruling party and Washington. The trip underscores deepening fault lines within Taiwan's political establishment over how to manage relations with Beijing. Separately, NASA's Artemis II crew broke the record for the farthest distance humans have travelled from Earth.

sources.title: Taiwan opposition leader visits China for expected meeting with Xi Jinping
JD Vance due in Hungary to back Orban's re-election bid
Vance heads to Budapest to shore up Orban’s support before Sunday vote
Artemis II astronauts break record for farthest human travel from Earth

ai.headline: OpenAI Faces Internal Trust Crisis While Mapping AI Economy
ai.body:
OpenAI insiders are openly questioning Sam Altman's leadership, with sources describing a culture of distrust around the CEO as the company pushes deeper into commercial territory. The tension is notable given the scale of ambitions on display: OpenAI published a policy vision this week calling for public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek to manage AI's economic disruption.

Meanwhile, a group of OpenAI alumni have been quietly deploying a fund that could reach $100 million, backing early-stage AI ventures outside the company's orbit. The moves suggest a widening gap between OpenAI's public optimism and the private skepticism of people who built it.

sources.title: “The problem is Sam Altman”: OpenAI Insiders don’t trust CEO
OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund

ai.headline: Iran Threatens Stargate Data Centers as AI Infrastructure Becomes a Target
ai.body:
Iran has issued explicit threats against OpenAI's Stargate data centers, including a facility under development in Abu Dhabi. The threats arrive as geopolitical tensions around AI infrastructure sharpen, with large-scale compute buildouts in the Gulf making tempting symbolic targets.

Anthropic moved in the opposite direction this week, expanding its partnership with Google and Broadcom to secure next-generation compute capacity. The contrast is stark: one frontier lab faces threats to its infrastructure while another locks in the supply chains to build more of it. Physical AI infrastructure is no longer an abstraction. It is a geopolitical asset.

sources.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
Iran threatens OpenAI’s Stargate data center in Abu Dhabi
Anthropic expands partnership with Google and Broadcom for next-gen compute

ai.headline: Robotics AI Hits 99% Reliability in Real Production Tasks
ai.body:
Generalist's GEN-1 robotics model has reached 99% task reliability across a range of physical jobs - folding boxes, repairing vacuums - marking a meaningful threshold for deploying AI in unstructured industrial settings. Previous generalist models struggled to clear the reliability bar that manufacturers actually require.

The result matters because it shifts the conversation from lab demos to production viability. Combined with MIT Technology Review's reporting on the difficulty of measuring AI's real impact on employment, the timing is pointed. Reliable physical AI is no longer theoretical, and the data to understand what that means for workers remains thin.

sources.title: From folding boxes to fixing vacuums, GEN-1 robotics model hits 99% reliability
The one piece of data that could actually shed light on your job and AI

ai.headline: AI Singer Floods iTunes Chart; Google Ships Offline Dictation Quietly
ai.body:
An AI-generated artist named Eddie Dalton now holds eleven spots on the iTunes singles chart, despite having no human behind the name. The chart takeover is the clearest sign yet that AI-generated music can game streaming and download platforms at scale, raising urgent questions for labels and platforms about what counts as a legitimate release.

On the tools side, Google quietly shipped an offline-first AI dictation app for iOS, competing directly with local speech-to-text projects like Ghost Pepper, an open-source macOS tool that surfaced on Hacker News this week. The pattern is familiar: independent developers ship something scrappy, then a platform giant follows with a polished version.

sources.title: AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
Show HN: Ghost Pepper – Local hold-to-talk speech-to-text for macOS

btc.headline: Bitcoin slides toward $68K while ETF inflows hit February high
btc.body:
Bitcoin is caught between two opposing signals. Spot prices dropped toward $68,000 as whale selling accelerated and retail demand softened, yet Bitcoin ETFs recorded their strongest inflows since February - suggesting institutional buyers are treating the dip as an entry point rather than an exit.

Meanwhile, Strategy continues to absorb nearly three times the new BTC being mined, tightening available supply. Geopolitical noise added a layer of uncertainty, with major crypto assets holding relatively steady as Trump set a Tuesday deadline for an Iran nuclear deal. Markets appear to be waiting rather than panicking.

sources.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows hit highest level since February
Bitcoin, ether, solana hold steady as Trump sets Tuesday night deadline for Iran deal
Bitcoin drops toward $68,000 as demand weakens and whales sell
Bitcoin may hit $110K as Strategy absorbs nearly 3x new BTC supply

btc.headline: SEC drafts crypto fundraising rules as safe harbor heads to White House
btc.body:
US crypto regulation is moving fast on two fronts. SEC Chair Atkins said the agency is close to releasing a dedicated crypto fundraising framework - effectively a 'Reg Crypto' - designed to give token issuers clearer rules for raising capital legally.

Separately, a crypto market safe harbor proposal has landed at the White House for final review. Together, the moves signal a deliberate pivot away from the enforcement-first posture of recent years toward something closer to a structured rulebook. How much flexibility that rulebook actually offers fundraisers remains the open question.

sources.title: Crypto market safe harbor lands at White House for review
SEC close to putting out 'reg crypto' for fundraising questions, Chair Atkins says

btc.headline: Bitcoin miners face power competition as Anthropic signs gigawatt AI deal
btc.body:
Bitcoin miners have a new rival for cheap electricity. Anthropic signed a multi-gigawatt compute deal that will pull significant power capacity toward AI infrastructure, directly competing with miners who depend on the same low-cost energy sources to stay profitable.

The pressure compounds an already difficult environment post-halving. AI data centers can typically pay more per megawatt than miners can justify at current BTC prices, giving utilities a financial incentive to prioritize them. Grayscale added a separate wrinkle, arguing that Bitcoin's quantum computing vulnerability is less a technical problem than a social coordination one - meaning the harder challenge is getting the network to agree on a fix, not building one.

sources.title: Bitcoin’s quantum challenges are ‘more social than technical’: Grayscale
Bitcoin miners face a new rival for cheap power as Anthropic signs multi-gigawatt compute deal

btc.headline: Onchain data convicts terrorism financiers as Solana tightens DeFi security
btc.body:
Blockchain forensics firm TRM Labs confirmed that onchain evidence played a central role in convicting three terrorism financiers in Indonesia - one of the clearest real-world demonstrations yet of crypto's traceability as a law enforcement tool.

On the security side, the Solana Foundation launched new tools and an incident response network aimed at reducing DeFi exploit damage as attacks on the ecosystem continue. Aave also saw a shakeup: risk provider Chaos Labs announced its departure, saying the decision was not made quickly. The exits and upgrades together reflect a DeFi sector still working out who is responsible when things go wrong.

sources.title: Onchain evidence was key to convicting 3 terrorism financiers: TRM Labs
Solana Foundation looks to beef up DeFi security as attacks continue
Chaos Labs taps out as Aave's risk provider, decision ‘not made in haste’

food.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod
food.description: Silky, caramelised fish fillets marinated in white miso, mirin, and sake then broiled until lacquered and golden
food.tip: Marinate for at least 24 hours — the longer the miso sits on the fish, the deeper the umami crust when it hits the heat

exercise.name: Tempo Staircase Run
exercise.equipment: A staircase of at least 3 flights or an outdoor stadium/hill with steps
exercise.description: Alternate between walking down slowly for active recovery and running up at 80% effort, increasing your floor target by one flight every three rounds to build lactate threshold without a gym

learn.topic: The Ship of Theseus and Personal Identity Over Time
learn.description: The ancient paradox asks whether an object that has had all its components replaced remains fundamentally the same object — philosophers from Hobbes to Parfit have extended this to human consciousness, asking whether the 'you' of ten years ago is literally the same person as you today.
learn.why: Derek Parfit's modern take in 'Reasons and Persons' dismantles intuitions about selfhood in ways that quietly reshape how you think about memory, responsibility, and why you bother planning for your future self at all
